title: Rewrite permission/2 as pattern-matched function heads with pin-binding
prompt: Refactor MyApp.RoleRouter.permission/2 from test_fixtures/cond_role_router.ex as multi-clause function heads. The interesting case is `user.role == "editor" and resource.owner_id == user.id` — this needs pinning. Use `def permission(%{role: "editor", id: uid}, %{owner_id: uid})` to pattern-match the identity across both arguments. Handle nil user returning `:denied`, admin returning `:full`, editor-as-owner returning `:write`, editor not owning returning `:read`, viewer returning `:read`, catch-all `:denied`. Output only the `permission/2` function inside `defmodule MyApp.RoleRouter do ... end`.
tier_rationale: The cross-argument pinning trick (same bound variable appearing in both parameters) is the multi-step insight. Sonnet typically pattern-matches the role but leaves the owner-id check in the body.
